newsSinceJun6: No material new news since June 6. The ZONE 2026 launches (Nucleus device-management platform, Workcloud IO/BI AI suites, Apera AI venture investment) were June 1-4 and the BNP Paribas PT raise to $370 was June 5 - all pre-window. Since then the stock has simply bled back the post-conference pop (~$253 June 3 -> $232 June 5 -> $227.10 June 9, -2.55% today) on no fundamental driver; YTD now -8.5%.

sources.label: Zebra ZONE 2026 software/AI launch press release (June 2)
StockTitan - Zebra debuts Nucleus, AI Workcloud suite
Public.com ZBRA analyst targets (BNP Paribas $370, June 5)
Simply Wall St - Nucleus AI platform and recent price action

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's down move already reconciled the adjusted-score gap; nothing genuinely new since June 6. The continued give-back to $227 (-8.5% YTD) with the operational-data thesis intact - and Nucleus/Workcloud actually reinforcing the clean-data endpoint angle - marginally improves entry asymmetry, worth a small positive drift but not another rank move.
verdicts.bull: Nucleus and the Workcloud AI suites deepen Zebra's role as the data-capture and device-management layer that physical-world AI depends on, and the stock now sits ~10% below its June 3 level on no negative news.
verdicts.neutral: A $11.9B incumbent with sell-side Strong Buy coverage and a $330 median target is not hidden; the alpha case still hinges on a warehouse/retail capex refresh that has not yet shown up in numbers.
verdicts.bear: Hardware-led model stays hostage to logistics capex cycles; if the refresh slips again, the AI-software narrative from ZONE will not carry the multiple and the slide from $253 continues.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Evidence of enterprise device refresh / Q2 print converting ZONE 2026 software launches into attach-rate and recurring-revenue growth.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Warehouse and retail capex stays frozen, leaving Zebra a cyclical hardware name with an AI label.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict was removal from the robotics alpha list and nothing since June 6 rebuilds the robotics case - the Skild sale stripped out direct robotics upside and the ZONE news is frontline software, not robotics. Continue migrating the name down/off this page; the small Apera AI venture stake (June 2, pre-window) is not a robotics thesis.
verdicts.bull: The Skild transaction proved Zebra's robotics assets had real value, and the Apera AI machine-vision stake keeps a toehold in industrial automation optionality.
verdicts.neutral: Zebra is now a sensing/data-capture company adjacent to robotics rather than a robotics play; its value to AI portfolios lives on the latent-nodes page, not here.
verdicts.bear: Post-Skild, robotics exposure is residual venture stakes and machine-vision peripherals; holding a $12B diversified hardware name at #19 on a robotics alpha list crowds out purer, smaller bottleneck names.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A new robotics-specific design win or partnership that would re-establish direct exposure (none visible).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued listing decay as the market fully prices Zebra ex-robotics, dragging the page's alpha integrity.
